title: Topology Declarations, Property Equivalence, Policies, and Ownership · Contract Lens: Topology Migration
summary: Multiple services declare the same queue with different durability and arguments, hardcode every operational x-argument, delete shared topology at startup, and turn precondition failures into reconnect loops. Planned artifact: Add and verify the topology migration increment in versioned topology declaration and ownership manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Migrate incompatible topology with versioned names, dual bindings, drain evidence, producer and consumer ordering, and rollback.
Place operator-changeable TTL, limits, dead lettering, delivery limits, and queue behavior in policies instead of immutable client x-arguments where supported.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Topology Migration · Theory Topology Migration
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 3551 in a recovery drill with stale callbacks from an old channel generation. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to protect property equivalence and route mutable operations through policy or versioned migration; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate incompatible topology with versioned names, dual bindings, drain evidence, producer and consumer ordering, and rollback.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Build Topology Migration evidence · Theory Topology Migration
